action.content:
You are playing a text adventure game. I will describe what you can perceive in the game. You write the single action you want to take in the game starting with >. Only reply with an action.
For example:
> open cupboard

You can have up to two objects in your inventory at the same time.

Your goal for this game is: Put the pillow on the counter, the book on the shelf and the plate on the table.

Once you have achieved your goal, write "> done" to end the game.

You are in a broom closet now. There are a mop and a broom. The mop is on the floor. The broom is on the floor. There is a passage to a hallway here.
action.label: context

*(Note: You currently have a plate and a potted plant in your inventory. You need to drop one of these items before you can take the pillow, or you need to know where the pillow is located first.)*

action.content:
> go to pantry

*(Rationale: You need to find the pillow and the shelf. The pantry is the only remaining unexplored room, and shelves are commonly found in pantries. Going there will allow you to locate the missing items and complete your inventory capacity.)*
